name: NVIDIA Corporation
note: Founder-CEO since 1993; world's most valuable company on AI/Rubin-platform boom

name: Apple Inc.
note: Sitting CEO; Apple announced Apr 20, 2026 that John Ternus becomes CEO Sept 1, 2026, with Cook moving to executive chairman

name: Alphabet Inc.
note: Google CEO since 2015, Alphabet CEO since Dec 2019; 2026 pay package tied to Waymo/Wing moonshots

name: Microsoft Corporation
note: CEO since 2014 and chairman since 2021; repositioning Microsoft/OpenAI alliance amid intensified AI competition

name: Amazon.com, Inc.
note: Succeeded founder Jeff Bezos in 2021; defending a $200B 2026 AI capex program

name: Broadcom Inc.
note: CEO since 2006 (Avago); custom AI chip boom (incl. Anthropic partnership), targets >$100B AI chip revenue in 2027

name: Space Exploration Technologies Corp.
note: Founder-CEO; largest-ever IPO June 12, 2026 (Nasdaq: SPCX, ~$1.77T valuation); Musk keeps ~82% voting power via Class B shares and stock has slipped back to the $135 IPO price

name: Meta Platforms, Inc.
note: Founder-CEO with dual-class voting control; betting on Meta Superintelligence Labs and possible cloud business

name: Tesla, Inc.
note: CEO since 2008; now also CEO of newly public SpaceX, keeping attention-split concerns alive as Tesla pivots to AI/robotics/energy

name: Berkshire Hathaway Inc.
note: Recent succession: Abel became President and CEO Jan 1, 2026, ending Warren Buffett's 60-year run; Buffett remains chairman

name: Eli Lilly and Company
note: Chair and CEO since 2017; steered Lilly to ~$1T market cap on GLP-1/obesity franchise, oral orforglipron launching 2026

name: Micron Technology, Inc.
note: CEO since 2017; stock up ~9x in a year on AI memory/HBM boom, crossed $1T market cap, $250B US fab expansion announced July 2026

name: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
note: CEO since 2006; succession race narrowed June 2026 to co-presidents Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh after Marianne Lake's exit — Dimon expects ~3 more years

name: Walmart Inc.
note: Recent succession: Furner (32-year Walmart veteran, ex-Walmart US CEO) took over Feb 1, 2026 as Doug McMillon retired

name: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.
note: Chair and CEO since 2014; ~40x stock turnaround, keynoted CES 2026 on AI roadmap

name: Visa Inc.
note: CEO since Feb 2023 (succeeded Al Kelly); elected US-China Business Council board chair June 2026

name: Exxon Mobil Corporation
note: Chairman and CEO since Jan 2017; navigating Iran-war oil price spikes and public friction with Trump over Venezuela

name: Johnson & Johnson
note: CEO since Jan 2022, chairman since 2023; on track for first-ever $100B revenue year in 2026

name: Intel Corporation
note: Recent succession: ex-Cadence chief appointed March 18, 2025 after Gelsinger's ouster, driving foundry-led turnaround; Craig Barratt elected independent board chair May 2026

name: Mastercard Incorporated
note: CEO since Jan 2021 (succeeded Ajay Banga); reshuffled leadership June 2026, promoting Ling Hai to CFO

name: Applied Materials, Inc.
note: Long-tenured CEO (since Sept 2013); no succession announced; calls this 'the greatest time ever' for semis (May 2026)

name: Cisco Systems, Inc.
note: Chair & CEO 11+ years; steered shift from hardware to software/subscriptions; active as of June 2026

name: Bank of America Corporation
note: Longest-tenured big-bank CEO; Feb 2026 co-president reshuffle (Athanasia, DeMare, Borthwick) set up succession race, but he plans to stay for years

name: AbbVie Inc.
note: Recent succession: took over from Richard Gonzalez July 2024, added chairman July 2025

name: Caterpillar Inc.
note: Recent succession: 28-year CAT veteran, CEO May 1 2025 succeeding Jim Umpleby, elected board chairman April 2026

name: Lam Research Corporation
note: CEO since Jan 2018; Feb 2026 leadership shuffle was COO-level (Varadarajan in, Lord retired) — Archer unchanged

name: Costco Wholesale Corporation
note: Succeeded Craig Jelinek Jan 2024; rose from forklift driver, no college degree; still CEO as of June 2026

name: Oracle Corporation
note: Co-CEOs since Sept 2025; Safra Catz moved to executive vice chair, Larry Ellison remains chairman/CTO; stock well off its 2025 AI-frenzy highs

name: UnitedHealth Group Incorporated
note: Boomerang CEO: returned May 2025 after Andrew Witty stepped down (prior CEO 2006-2017); also board chair; testified to Congress Jan 2026

name: GE Aerospace
note: GE CEO since Oct 2018; has led GE Aerospace as standalone since April 2024 spin; employment agreement extended through end-2027

name: Chevron Corporation
note: Chairman & CEO since Feb 2018; board waived age-65 retirement rule; succession talks underway but no successor named as of mid-2026

name: Morgan Stanley
note: Succeeded James Gorman Jan 2024, added chairman Jan 2025; delivered record Q1 2026 results

name: The Coca-Cola Company
note: Very recent succession: former COO became CEO March 31 2026, succeeding James Quincey (now executive chairman)

name: The Procter & Gamble Company
note: Recent succession: former COO became CEO Jan 1 2026, succeeding Jon Moeller (now executive chairman); P&G lifer since 1989

name: The Home Depot, Inc.
note: Chair, president & CEO since March 2022 (chairman since Oct 2022); no succession announced

name: The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc.
note: Chairman & CEO since Oct 2018 (chairman 2019); earlier activist/board noise has faded after strong 2025

name: Palantir Technologies Inc.
note: Founder-CEO (co-founded 2003, CEO since 2004); stock down ~27% in 2026 despite ~85% YoY revenue growth; outspoken vs OpenAI/Anthropic

name: Netflix, Inc.
note: Co-CEO structure since Jan 2023 (Sarandos co-CEO since 2020); founder Reed Hastings left the board June 2026; share price reflects 2025 10-for-1 split

name: Merck & Co., Inc.
note: Chairman & CEO (CEO July 2021, chairman Dec 2022); navigating Keytruda patent-cliff narrative

name: KLA Corporation
note: One of the longest-tenured semicap CEOs (since Jan 2006); no successor announced as of mid-2026

name: Palo Alto Networks, Inc.
note: Chairman & CEO since June 2018 (ex-Google/SoftBank); active in July 2026 press (CNBC interview July 9, 2026)

name: GE Vernova Inc.

name: Philip Morris International Inc.
note: CEO since May 2021; title redesignated 'Group CEO PMI' effective Jan 1, 2026 in an org restructure

name: Texas Instruments Incorporated

name: Dell Technologies Inc.
note: Founder-CEO (founded 1984; CEO again since 2007); confirmed Chairman & CEO in FY2026 proxy

name: Wells Fargo & Company
note: CEO since Oct 2019, added chairman Oct 2025; led bank out of Fed asset cap (lifted 2025)

name: RTX Corporation
note: President & CEO since May 2024 (succeeded Greg Hayes), elected chairman April 2025

name: American Express Company
note: Chairman & CEO since Feb 2018; age 67, says no plans to retire, no successor named (May 2026)

name: Sandisk Corporation

name: Citigroup Inc.

name: Arista Networks, Inc.

name: CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc.
note: Co-founder & CEO since founding in 2011; retained role through and after the July 2024 outage

name: T-Mobile US, Inc.

name: Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc.

name: International Business Machines Corporation

name: Amgen Inc.
note: Chairman & CEO; CEO since 2012, chairman since 2013; signed 2025 shareholder letter March 2026, big MariTide capex

name: Amphenol Corporation

name: Analog Devices, Inc.

name: McDonald's Corporation

name: Marvell Technology, Inc.

name: PepsiCo, Inc.

name: Verizon Communications Inc.

name: The Charles Schwab Corporation

name: BlackRock, Inc.

name: Western Digital Corporation

name: The Boeing Company

name: Union Pacific Corporation
note: CEO since August 2023; leading the pending ~$72B Norfolk Southern merger now under STB review

name: The Walt Disney Company

name: The TJX Companies, Inc.

name: Interactive Brokers Group, Inc.

name: Welltower Inc.

name: Gilead Sciences, Inc.
note: Chairman & CEO since March 2019, ex-Roche Pharmaceuticals chief; no transition announced

name: Deere & Company
note: Chairman & CEO since November 2019; new CFO appointed May 2026 but no CEO transition announced

name: Abbott Laboratories
note: CEO since March 2020, chairman since December 2021; bought ~$2M of ABT stock personally in 2026 on share weakness

name: Blackstone Inc.

name: AppLovin Corporation
note: Founder-CEO since the company's 2011-2012 founding; handed off the board chair role in April 2026 but remains CEO

name: Southern Copper Corporation

name: Corning Incorporated

name: AT&T Inc.

name: Uber Technologies, Inc.

name: Danaher Corporation
note: Internal promotion (ex-Life Sciences EVP), President & CEO since Sept 2020; Danaher Business System steward

name: Booking Holdings Inc.

name: Pfizer Inc.

name: Intuitive Surgical, Inc.

name: Prologis, Inc.

name: Salesforce, Inc.

name: CVS Health Corporation

name: S&P Global Inc.

name: Capital One Financial Corporation

name: Stryker Corporation

name: Fortinet, Inc.

name: Parker-Hannifin Corporation

name: Bristol-Myers Squibb Company

name: Starbucks Corporation

name: The Progressive Corporation

name: Lockheed Martin Corporation
